case_title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. MITCHELL ENGINEERING, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al

docket_entries.description: NTC OF MTN UNDER CCP 2031.210 TO COMPEL DF MITCHELL ENGINEERING TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES & DOCUMENTS RESPONSIVE TO 1ST SET OF REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS & FOR SANCTIONS AGAINST DF & ITS COUNSEL; DECLARATIONS, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, SEPARATE STATEMENT FILED BY PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO HEARING SET FOR NOV-29-2007 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPT 610

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT MITCHELL ENGINEERING'S DEMURRER TO CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. THE ISSUE RAISED RE LIQUIDATED DAMAGES IS NOT APPROPRIATE FOR DEMURRER. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES DAMAGES, GENERAL, COMPENSATORY AND CONSEQUENTIAL. AS SUCH A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS ALLEGED. Ntc Of Mtn Under Ccp 2031.210 To Compel Df Mitchell Engineering To Provide Further Responses & Documents Responsive To 1st Set Of Requests For Production Of Documents & For Sanctions Against Df & Its Counsel; Declarations
Off Calendar Jan-25-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearance:
David L. Norman, Deputy City Attorney, for plaintiff and moving party: City and County of San Francisco
Alexander Bannon, Esq., for defendant and opposing party: Mitchell Engineering and Lumbermens Mutual Casualty Company
Court Reporter: Diane L. Freeman, CSR #5884
AFTER HEARING, TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents is Granted in part and denied in part.
The motion is moot as to requests #: 19-21, 24, 26-29 as defendant has agreed to produce responsive documents. The motion is moot as to requests #: 15, 18, and 30 as defendant has stated that there are no documents. Defendant shall prepare a separate declaration under penalty of perjury verifying said representation.

The motion is granted as to requests: 1 - 5, 7-12, 14, 16, 17, 22, 23, 25 provided that
Counsel for either party may not disclose, or permit to be disclosed, any documents produced in response to the requests for purposes unrelated to the instant litigation or any administrative proceedings unless specifically permitted to do so by the court after a hearing and a showing of good cause.

Notwithstanding the above, counsel for either party may disclose or permit to be disclosed the records produced in discovery to persons employed by counsel to assist in the preparation of the case, if that disclosure is required for that preparation. Persons provided this information by counsel shall be informed that further dissemination of the information constitutes a violation of a court order.

As to requests #6 and 13, the motion is granted in part at this time subject to the terms of the protective order set forth above. Defendant is ordered to produce responsive documents that are specific to the project. Defendant may redact information unrelated to the project at issue. Parties to submit Protective Order. Compliance by February 25, 2008. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-05-2007 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MITCHELL ENGINEERING'S DEMURRER TO CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Louise S. Simpson, Deputy City Atty 554-3929 for pltf & opposing party; no other appearances.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - OVERRULED, 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. THE ISSUE RAISED RE LIQUIDATED DAMAGES IS NOT APPROPRIATE FOR DEMURRER. THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES DAMAGES, GENERAL, COMPENSATORY AND CONSEQUENTIAL. AS SUCH A CLAIM FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT IS ALLEGED. Order signed in open court. (302/PJM/PB) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600
